_meta.description: Article lifecycle, subject tree, search, and linking to cases.

mcq.q: What is the purpose of a Subject Tree in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: To define escalation paths for SLA violations
To categorise cases and knowledge articles using a hierarchical classification structure
To map routing rules to queues
To track the resolution steps of a case
mcq.explanation: The Subject Tree provides a hierarchical taxonomy for classifying cases and knowledge articles. It enables consistent categorisation and supports filtering and reporting across the organisation.
wrongExplanations: Escalation paths for SLA violations are handled by SLA KPIs and actions, not the subject tree.
Routing rules map cases to queues based on conditions — subject tree is a classification tool.
Resolution steps are tracked on the case itself via the resolution activity — not via subjects.

mcq.q: Which Dynamics 365 Customer Service feature stores reusable answers that agents can link to cases to assist with resolution?
mcq.explanation: Knowledge Articles are structured, searchable documents that capture solutions, procedures, and FAQs. Agents can link them to cases and customers can find them in the customer portal.
wrongExplanations: Quick Replies are short pre-written messages used in live chat channels — not structured solution articles.
Email Templates are pre-formatted emails for outbound communication — not knowledge content.
Macros are agent productivity scripts that automate sequences of actions — not knowledge articles.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each knowledge article status to its meaning in the article lifecycle.
zones: Article is being authored — not yet submitted
Article is awaiting review by a knowledge manager
Article has passed review and is ready to publish
Article is live and visible to agents and customers
Article has been retired from active use

correctMapping.Draft: Article is being authored — not yet submitted
correctMapping.In Review: Article is awaiting review by a knowledge manager
correctMapping.Approved: Article has passed review and is ready to publish
correctMapping.Published: Article is live and visible to agents and customers
correctMapping.Archived: Article has been retired from active use

dragdrop.explanation: Knowledge Articles follow a Draft to In Review to Approved to Published to Archived lifecycle. Only Published articles are visible to agents and customers in search results.

casestudy.title: Contoso Support Centre Setup
casestudy.scenario: Contoso has recently licensed Dynamics 365 Customer Service and is setting up the system from scratch. The support centre has the following requirements: 1. All inbound emails to support@contoso.com must automatically create cases 2. Cases must be categorised using a standard product taxonomy 3. Agents should only be able to edit cases they personally own 4. Supervisors must be able to view and reassign all cases across the team 5. Cases must be resolved within 4 hours of creation, with a warning at 3 hours 6. Agents must confirm the customer was notified before resolving a case The admin team is configuring all of these requirements before going live.

questions.q: Contoso wants to classify cases using a standard product taxonomy such as Hardware > Laptops > Battery. Which feature supports this?
options: Category field with an option set of product names
Subject Tree configured with a hierarchical product taxonomy
Business Process Flow stages for each product
Tag field on the Case entity
questions.explanation: The Subject Tree supports multi-level hierarchical classification. Configuring it with Hardware > Laptops > Battery provides agents with a structured taxonomy and enables filtering and reporting by product category.
wrongExplanations: An option set Category field is flat — it does not support the multi-level hierarchy required.
Business Process Flows define workflow stages — they are not classification taxonomies.
A Tag field is informal text — it does not provide a governed hierarchical taxonomy.
